question_text: If the Earth's axis tilt were increased from 23.5° to 45°, how would this affect the seasons?

options.label: A
options.text: Seasons would be less extreme, with milder summers and winters

options.label: B
options.text: Seasons would remain unchanged because tilt direction matters, not the angle

options.label: C
options.text: Seasons would be much more extreme, with much hotter summers and colder winters

options.label: D
options.text: There would be no seasons at all

explanation: A larger tilt would mean the hemisphere tilted towards the Sun would receive much more direct energy (very hot summers), and the hemisphere tilted away would receive much less (very cold winters). The angle of tilt directly determines how extreme the seasons are.
